name: SegmentProgressBar
description: A progress bar divided into discrete segments that fill based on percentage. Provides a visual representation of progress through distinct steps or stages rather than a continuous bar.
usage_guidelines: Set segments to match your workflow steps.
Customize filled segment color via filledClass.
Adjust gap and height for visual preferences.

segments.description: Total number of segments to display in the progress bar.

percent.description: Completion percentage (0-100). Determines how many segments are filled.

filledClass.description: Tailwind CSS class for filled segment background color.

className.description: Additional CSS classes applied to each segment.

height.description: Height of each segment in pixels.

styling_info.visual_description: A horizontal row of rounded rectangular segments with equal width. Filled segments use the filledClass color (default emerald-500), unfilled segments are gray-200 (gray-700 in dark mode). Segments have rounded corners and configurable gaps.

Do: Use for multi-step processes or wizards
Match segment count to actual workflow steps
Use semantic colors for different states
Combine with step labels for clarity
Dont: Don't use for continuous progress (use Progress component)
Don't use too many segments (becomes hard to read)
Don't forget to update percent as steps complete
Don't use without context about what segments represent
